content.full_text:
Q2-537

CITY OF DUBLIN
"Celebrating 10 Years Of Cityhood 1982-1992"

PO. Box 2340, Dublin, California 94568 • City Offices, 100 Civic Plaza, Dublin, California 94568

October 5, 1992

OCT 0 8 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Commission:

The Dublin City Council has called a special election to be held
in the City of Dublin on Tuesday, January 5, 1993. There will be
two measures on the ballot. Measure A deals with a referendum,
and Measure B is a City Council sponsored initiative. Both
measures are related to the topic of a General Plan Amendment and
Specific Plan for western Dublin.

In putting together my election calendar, questions have surfaced
as a result of holidays which coincide with various filing
deadlines.

In accordance with the modified filing schedule for all elections
held on a date other than June 2nd or November 3rd, the first
pre-election statement will be due on November 26th. This is
Thanksgiving Day and the City of Dublin also observes the
following day as a City holiday. Is Friday, November 27th also
an official State holiday which would therefore mean that the
deadline is extended until Monday, November 30th? What if a
holiday is not a State holiday but rather only a City holiday?

The second pre-election statement will be due on December 24th
which is Christmas Eve, a holiday observed by the City of Dublin.
Can the filing date for this statement be extended to Monday,
December 28th?

With regard to the 24 hour late contribution/expenditure
reporting requirement during the last 16 days before the
election, 9 of these 16 days fall on either weekends or holidays.
Since the extension provision to the next regular business day
does not apply to the 24 hour reports, and since City Offices
will be closed, and since I as Filing Officer will not be here to
receive the reports, what happens?

Also, as an example, if a committee receives a contribution or
makes an expenditure which is reportable on Christmas Eve at
5:00 p.m., and they must file by guaranteed overnight delivery,
but the post office is closed and they cannot get a receipt until
Monday, what happens? If it is sent Federal Express doesn't
someone have to sign for it? The same scenario could occur on
New Year's Eve.

Administration (510) 833-6650 • City Council (510) 833-6605 • Finance (510) 833-6640 • Building Inspection (510) 833-6620
Code Enforcement (510) 833-6620 • Engineering (510) 833-6630 • Planning (510) 833-6610
Police (510) 833-6670 • Public Works (510) 833-6630 • Recreation (510) 833-6645

Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 2

Since the special election will be held the first Tuesday in
January, and with the 2 weekends immediately preceding the
election being 4 day weekends, I could conceivably see this type
of a situation occurring.

One other informational point: the City of Dublin receives the
majority of its mail in a post office box. Mail directed to the
street address is delivered only during work hours as the city
has no mail slot into the building.

I would appreciate it very much if you could address my questions
as soon as possible. If you have any questions, or need
additional information, please feel free to contact me at (510)
833-6650.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Kay Reck, CMC
City Clerk
City of Dublin

cc: City Attorney

sections.question: The Dublin City Council has called a special election for January 5, 1993, with two ballot measures. Questions have surfaced regarding filing deadlines that coincide with holidays: (1) Is Friday, November 27th an official State holiday extending the November 26th filing deadline to Monday, November 30th? What if a holiday is only a City holiday rather than a State holiday? (2) Can the December 24th filing date for the second pre-election statement be extended to Monday, December 28th? (3) With 9 of the last 16 days before the election falling on weekends or holidays, and the extension provision not applying to 24-hour reports, what happens when City Offices are closed and the Filing Officer is unavailable? (4) If a committee receives a contribution or makes an expenditure reportable on Christmas Eve at 5:00 p.m. and must file by guaranteed overnight delivery, but the post office is closed and a receipt cannot be obtained until Monday, what is the requirement? Does Federal Express require a signature? Could the same scenario occur on New Year's Eve?
sections.question_synthetic: The Dublin City Clerk seeks guidance on how filing deadlines for campaign statements and 24-hour contribution/expenditure reports should be handled when they fall on state holidays, city-only holidays, weekends, or when city offices are closed, particularly in the context of a January 1993 special election.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a city official to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains detailed questions about filing deadline extensions but no conclusion or advice from the FPPC. OCR quality is fair with some scanning artifacts but content is clearly readable.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The Dublin City Council has called a special election for January 5, 1993, with two ballot measures. Questions have surfaced regarding filing deadlines that coincide with holidays: (1) Is Friday, November 27th an official State holiday extending the November 26th filing deadline to Monday, November 30th? What if a holiday is only a City holiday rather than a State holiday? (2) Can the December 24th filing date for the second pre-election statement be extended to Monday, December 28th? (3) With 9 of the last 16 days before the election falling on weekends or holidays, and the extension provision not applying to 24-hour reports, what happens when City Offices are closed and the Filing Officer is unavailable? (4) If a committee receives a contribution or makes an expenditure reportable on Christmas Eve at 5:00 p.m. and must file by guaranteed overnight delivery, but the post office is closed and a receipt cannot be obtained until Monday, what is the requirement? Does Federal Express require a signature? Could the same scenario occur on New Year's Eve?

embedding.summary: The City Clerk of Dublin, California requests FPPC guidance on how to handle campaign finance filing deadlines that coincide with holidays and closures around a scheduled January 5, 1993 special election.
